title: OIDC CI/CD trust-condition conformance vector suite
description: A file of test vectors, each answering: does subject S satisfy trust condition C, and is C safe?

suite.description: Stable identifier for this suite file, e.g. 'github-aws'.

version.description: Suite revision, bumped when vectors change.

id.description: Kebab-case, unique across the whole corpus.

issuer.description: Which CI system minted the subject.

subject.description: A concrete OIDC token subject claim value (no wildcards).

claims.description: The full OIDC token claim set the condition is evaluated against (gcp-cel addresses it as assertion.<name>; an AWS condition with claim != 'sub', e.g. 'aud', resolves its value here). Optional: vectors whose condition targets 'sub' may omit it and match on 'subject' alone. When present, claims.sub MUST equal 'subject'.

expect.description: Whether the subject satisfies the condition.

status.description: 'documented' = derived from cited docs; 'observed' = confirmed against a real issuer/cloud.

sources.description: Primary-source URLs. Every vector is cited; none ship on vibes.

consumer.description: Which cloud consumer's matching semantics apply. 'aws-all' models a full IAM Condition block: an AND of AWS string sub-conditions in 'of'.

claim.description: Token claim the condition targets (default 'sub'), same shape as claims-map keys. When not 'sub', the vector MUST carry a 'claims' object (the targeted claim may be deliberately absent from it to encode absent-key semantics).

pattern.description: The admin-written trust rule (may contain wildcards, per consumer). An array models an AWS multi-value condition (one key, several values, logical OR) and is only valid for the aws-* consumers; a single value is written as a plain string.

of.description: aws-all only: the ANDed sub-conditions of one IAM Condition block ('All context keys in a condition element block must resolve to true'). Each entry is an AWS string condition -- operators may differ per entry (StringEquals aud + StringLike sub) and each may use claim targeting and value lists.

grade.description: Human-authored safety grade for the condition; never computed by the matcher.

patterns.description: Catalog tags for the over-permission pattern(s), e.g. 'wildcard-repo'.
